reason:
Node 1.2.2 explicitly states 'Apply the single-constraint result iteratively' but declares NO dependencies on Node 1.2.1 (the single-constraint case). This is a critical structural gap:

1. MISSING DEPENDENCY: The entire proof of 1.2.2 rests on repeated application of the result proved in 1.2.1. Without this dependency declaration, if 1.2.1 is challenged or rejected, 1.2.2 will not be properly tainted. The dependency 1.2.1 must be explicitly declared.

2. HYPOTHESIS VERIFICATION AT EACH STEP: The proof says 'Apply the single-constraint result iteratively' but never verifies that the hypotheses of Node 1.2.1 are satisfied at each inductive step. Specifically, at step i, Node 1.2.1 is applied with K replaced by K_{i-1} and f replaced by f_i|_{K_{i-1}}. The proof must verify:
   (a) K_{i-1} is convex (true: intersection of convex sets is convex, but should be stated).
   (b) f_i restricted to K_{i-1} remains a lower semicontinuous generalized affine map (true: restriction of lsc to subspace topology preserves lsc, and restriction of generalized affine to convex subset preserves affinity on effective domain, but should be stated).
   (c) x is in K_i for all i (true: x is in C = K_m which is a subset of K_i for all i, but should be stated).

Without these verifications, the inductive step has an unjustified hypothesis gap.

FIX: Add an explicit dependency on 1.2.1, and either add sub-nodes or inline text verifying that the hypotheses of 1.2.1 are satisfied at each step i of the iteration (convexity of K_{i-1}, generalized affine property of f_i|_{K_{i-1}}, and membership x in K_i).
